text:
Australia's golden girl Jess Fox has taken on a fresh challenge outside of Canoe Slalom by linking up with a French beauty brand.

Australian Olympian Jess Fox has tackled the sporting world of Canoe Slalom and is now embracing French beauty brand L'Oréal Paris on the catwalk.

The three-time Olympic gold-medallist will be taking part in theWalk Your Worth Runwaycelebrating women's empowerment on Thursday October 24 at the Sydney Opera House.

The walk embodies L'Oréal's famous catchphrase "Because You're Worth It" and seeks to inspire women to meet their destiny with confidence.

The French-born athlete spoke to The Daily Telegraph in what's an exciting and nervous time ahead of her catwalk debut.

Fox will be joined by other well-known Australian figures with television personality Jessica Rowe, Paralympic swimmer Ellie Cole, model Riley Hemson, and actor Madeleine Madden in attendance.

"Walking a runway, especially in heels, is definitely outside my comfort zone, but the iconic Sydney Opera House backdrop makes it an unforgettable opportunity. I can’t wait to meet the other incredible women and celebrate being part of this movement," she said.

The Aussie superstar shared her excitement to work with the French brand, revealing how it takes her back to her childhood from when she was born in Marseille.

"L’Oréal Paris is a brand I’ve known and loved since I was a teenager buying my first mascara and lipstick," she said.

"Being born in France, I remember growing up seeing the ads in French and English, and that French connection makes it such a lovely fit."

Australian Brand Director of L'Oréal, Nadia Adelina, revealed to Nine Honey that the runway show brings together inspiring and diverse women who reflect a modern and inclusive vision of beauty.

"For L'Oréal Paris, beauty is about more than what you look like, and all of these women – led by Jessica Fox, Madeleine Madden, and Riley Hemson – are dedicated to uplifting and empowering every Australian woman to believe in her own inherent worth," she said.

Fox came home an absolute winner as she had her best Olympics to date, earning two gold medals through her performances in the Canoe Slalom K1 and the Canoe Slalom C1 during the 2024 Paris Olympic Games.

The Aussie golden girl is still keeping fit as she recently ran alongside Nedd Brockmann giving him much needed motivation during Nedd's Uncomfortable Challenge, where he ran 1,600 kilometres in 12 days raising money for homelessness.
